source: Learning Rsync - rsync configuration file: Supplementary daemon parameters and a recommended configuration

questions.q: The uid and gid parameters default to which user/group when the daemon runs as root?
questions.why: uid/gid default to nobody -- the account used to transfer files.

questions.q: What does `use chroot = yes` do (and what is the default)?
options: Locks (chroots) the module's root directory before transfer for security; default is yes
Runs the daemon as root; default no
questions.why: use chroot locks the root directory before transmitting; rsync defaults it to yes for security.
max connections default 0 means unrestricted; it pairs with a lock file when set.
timeout is seconds of no data before rsync exits; 0 means never.

questions.q: If a client matches BOTH hosts allow and hosts deny, what happens (and if it matches neither)?
options: Matching both -> denied; matching neither -> allowed
questions.why: A conflicting match denies the client; a client in neither list is allowed by default.

options: Storing complete file attributes without running the daemon as root
questions.why: fake super lets the daemon preserve full attributes without needing to run as root.

questions.q: How are multiple auth users written?
questions.why: Multiple virtual users in auth users are comma-separated.

options: Excludes that directory from the transfer
questions.why: exclude lists directories that should not be transferred.
